{"key_event":"May 1968 revolt and Gaullist reassertion","reasoning":"The Fifth Republic presidency, technocratic modernization, fiscal growth, and the end of the Algerian crisis pushed an already strong French state into very high capacity, though May 1968 showed limits to coercive and administrative control. Civil society also strengthened: unions, students, new movements, mass education, and the general strike demonstrated exceptional mobilizing power, even as executive-centered institutions constrained parliamentary and party intermediaries.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":8.8,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":9.1}
